statement: Fork of Fuchsia/Zircon is the kernel foundation
evidence_status: Confirmed by program decision; implementation evidence pending

statement: Portable service/product layers can span unrelated SoCs
evidence_status: Requires two native ports and conformance evidence

statement: POSIX is unnecessary as a native platform contract
evidence_status: Core workloads must be demonstrated through native APIs; compatibility remains optional

statement: Android/Linux can remain isolated to Pixel 9
evidence_status: Dependency lint and two non-Pixel backends must prove absence above adapters

statement: Pixel 9 bootloader unlock is available
evidence_status: Official Android documentation describes unlock behavior; exact Pixel SKU/operator must be verified before purchase

statement: Pixel 9 is an economical native target
evidence_status: Requires boot/debug/display/storage/power/camera dossier and legal rights

statement: Panfrost knowledge substantially solves Pixel 9 graphics
evidence_status: Open Mali userspace knowledge does not solve SoC display, power, firmware, IOMMU, or Agent OS driver integration

statement: Linux userspace cellular software can be wrapped unchanged
evidence_status: Native hardware/service contracts and kernel-facing work remain necessary; reference behavior may be reused lawfully

statement: Cellular data implies voice/IMS feasibility
evidence_status: Data, SMS, SIM/eSIM, emergency, voice and IMS are separate layers and certification paths

statement: Open phone-form-factor hardware can host native Agent OS
evidence_status: PinePhone Pro dossier and staged display/touch/audio/power/modem bring-up required

statement: A documented-board track lowers architectural risk
evidence_status: Official schematics/reference material and independent recovery/debug make first ports more controllable

statement: A camera-capable documented SoC can reach good image quality
evidence_status: Depends on sensor/module, ISP/control access, calibration, tuning and computational pipeline

statement: Published HDR+ research can be used as-is for Pixel quality
evidence_status: Research concepts are usable; production Pixel pipeline, tuning and vendor integration are not published as a portable stack

statement: A proprietary camera component can coexist with portable Agent OS
evidence_status: Acceptable only behind documented contracts with legal deployment/update/replacement rights

statement: All user secrets can be backed up and migrated
evidence_status: Hardware-bound and non-exportable secrets must be regenerated or re-enrolled

statement: No user input byte should ever be lost
evidence_status: Use semantic journaling and explicit secure/transient exclusions, not global raw-input capture

statement: An entity-first shell improves common workflows
evidence_status: Requires task-time, error, comprehension and retention studies against alternatives

statement: Agent autonomy is safe with capability mediation alone
evidence_status: Capabilities must combine with proposal review, effect classes, receipts, budgets, policy, evaluation and compensation

statement: CRDTs are the universal storage model
evidence_status: Use event logs, transactions, snapshots and CRDTs by data semantics

statement: A future ODM device can be deferred without architectural cost
evidence_status: Manufacturing test, provisioning, board packages and hardware service boundaries must be designed now

statement: Community support can materially expand hardware coverage
evidence_status: Requires reproducible kits, maintainer ownership, documentation, bounties and honest support levels

statement: Fairphone or Sony can provide a quality bridge target
evidence_status: Requires exact-device documentation, boot control, redistribution, camera/power dependencies and vendor response

statement: Two native SoC ports are enough to prove portability
evidence_status: They expose major leakage but not every future architecture; conformance and a third architecture remain desirable

statement: A custom carrier board reduces product risk
evidence_status: Works only after module/documentation/supply/manufacturing-test contracts are stable

statement: Entity-first navigation improves cross-domain task completion
evidence_status: Requires comparative usability studies against application-first baselines

statement: A provider-published action catalog can cover high-value workflows without unrestricted UI automation
evidence_status: Measure coverage and failure behavior across first-party and partner providers

statement: Semantic journaling can preserve useful recovery without recording raw secrets
evidence_status: Requires redaction, field classification, opt-out, and attack testing

statement: A portable camera service can separate application APIs from sensor/ISP backends
evidence_status: Prove on UVC/libcamera-like open backend and one quality-hardware backend

statement: Open ISP documentation can materially reduce camera bring-up risk
evidence_status: Benchmark PiSP or documented ISP path against black-box Pixel evidence

statement: A future ODM device can adopt the same service contracts without product-layer fork
evidence_status: Requires contract package, reference BSP and second-vendor portability review

statement: Clean-room separation can produce legally reviewable interoperability specifications
evidence_status: Counsel must approve jurisdiction, inputs, roles, records and outputs

statement: Agent autonomy can be bounded by capabilities, budgets, confirmation tiers and receipts
evidence_status: Adversarial tests must show no privilege amplification or silent irreversible effect

statement: Community participation can lower hardware-documentation and driver cost
evidence_status: Track accepted evidence, maintainers, vendor responses and reproducible ports

statement: The project can avoid public brand collision while retaining stable technical identifiers
evidence_status: AOS identifiers are neutral; public name still requires professional clearance
